title: JTBD — NAICS 531120 Lessors of Nonresidential Buildings

sections.title: 1. Derivation Context (Phase 1 recap)
sections.content:
| Item | Value |
|---|---|
| Industry | NAICS 531120 — Lessors of Nonresidential Buildings |
| CFJ | *Provide operationally-ready, leasable non-residential space with reliable building infrastructure and tenant services, under a long-term rental/lease contract, at a risk-adjusted yield that funds capex and asset appreciation.* |
| Offering-level | L5 system (anchored at `Battery Energy Storage System`) |
| Parent VN unit | `Building Energy Infrastructure` (L6d-a) — defines the job context |
| Grandparent | `Property Operations & Infrastructure` (L6d) |
| Route | B2B deep (landlord as immediate customer; tenant as beneficiary) |

Phase 1 (CFJ, Output Types, Segments, Process VN) already complete in `VN_lessors_of_nonresidential_buildings_sams.md`. This file executes **Phase 2 + Phase 3**.

---

sections.title: 2. Segments (from VN · circumstance-based)
sections.content:
| # | Segment | Defining circumstance |
|---|---|---|
| S1 | Logistics / Industrial Park Lessor | High-kW EV fleet charging + cold-dock loads; DSO MV feeder often constrained |
| S2 | Office / Campus Lessor | Moderate loads, tenant ESG / CSRD disclosure pressure, Mieterstrom potential |
| S3 | Retail Center Lessor | HVAC-driven summer peaks, anchor-tenant charging stations |
| S4 | Medical-Office / Healthcare-Adjacent Lessor | Resilience-critical; outage spoils diagnostics + liability |
| S5 | Data-Center Shell / Powered-Shell Lessor | Power-intensive, 99.999% uptime expectation, UPS replacement cycle |

Segments merge where alternatives are identical. **S1, S4, S5 activate BESS strongest** (see activation matrix in VN doc).

---

sections.title: 3. Phase 2 — Company Enters
sections.content:
### 3.1 VN Anchor

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| anchor_vn_unit | `Battery Energy Storage System` |
| anchor_level | L5 |
| parent_vn_unit | `Building Energy Infrastructure` |
| parent_level | L6a (sub-SoS under L6d) |
| anchor_type | PROCESS_VN (BESS is equipment that *serves* the building, not *inside* the leased output) |

### 3.2 Lens

Function verb from parent L6d-a job family:
> **Deliver continuous building services, energy, and automation**

Lens per segment: *"What buffering / shifting / firming of electrical energy does this lessor need at the grid-building interface?"*

### 3.3 Product Job Statement

> **"Help `Building Energy Infrastructure` to buffer, shift, and firm electrical energy flows at the building's grid interface to protect net operating income and tenant uptime under rising Netzentgelte and constrained DSO capacity."**

Abstraction test: **PASS** — ≥3 alternatives from different categories (BESS / flow battery / tariff renegotiation / genset + no-storage) qualify.

### 3.4 Alternatives (5)

| # | Alternative | UNSPSC | Category | Inherent trade-offs |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| A1 | Containerized Li-ion BESS (incumbent) | 26111711 | Same category | Fire hazard, thermal runaway, MV footprint 40–80 m²/MWh, 10–15% roundtrip loss |
| A2 | Vanadium flow battery | 26111700 | Different category / same outcome | 4× footprint, lower roundtrip (65–75%), but non-flammable, 20-yr cycle life |
| A3 | Diesel / gas standby genset | 26111607 | Different approach (resilience only, no peak-shave/arbitrage) | Fuel logistics, emissions permit, zero revenue stack, noise |
| A4 | Utility tariff renegotiation + load-shifting contracts | — | Workaround | No capex, no asset on balance sheet; savings capped at ~15%, no resilience, no revenue stack |
| A5 | Do nothing / accept peak charges + grid upgrade | — | Status quo | Zero P1 burden; NOI erodes as Netzentgelte escalate; grid upgrade cost can exceed €200k and take 2 yr |

**Existential alternatives:** A4 (tariff workaround) and A5 (status quo) — eliminate the BESS product category entirely if landlord sees no business case. These are the strongest competitors.

### 3.5 Scenarios (activate output types + edges)

| Scenario | Segment | Output type activated | BOM position in play |
|---|---|---|---|
| Sc1 — Logistics park retrofits EV fleet depot | S1 | Logistics warehouse | HPC dispensers, MV transformer, BESS container |
| Sc2 — Office campus under CSRD disclosure pressure | S2 | Office tower | PV generator, BESS, Mieterstrom meter |
| Sc3 — Retail center anchor adds HPC charging hub | S3 | Retail box | BESS (buffer), HPC dispensers |
| Sc4 — Med-office needs outage-ride-through + diesel displacement | S4 | Med-office | BESS, legacy genset (displaced) |
| Sc5 — Powered-shell data-center lessor lines up hybrid UPS/BESS | S5 | DC shell | BESS (UPS replacement), MV switchgear |

---

sections.title: 4. Stakeholder Roles
sections.content:
| Role | Who (in lessor context) | Pyramid levels relevant |
|---|---|---|
| Job Beneficiary | Tenant (receives reliable, increasingly-green power) | P2, P4, P5 |
| Job Executor | Property Manager / Facility Manager | P1 (all 5 stages), P2 |
| Job Overseer | Asset Manager / Portfolio Director | P1 (Acquisition, Maintenance), P2, P4 |
| Lifecycle Support | MEP / TGA consultant (specifier), EPC (installer), O&M provider, repowering partner | P1 own-stage |
| Purchase Executor | Investment Committee / CFO (REIT / fund) | P1 Acquisition only, P4 |
| Purchase Influencer | ESG consultant, insurance underwriter, debt provider, DSO grid engineer | own-P2, P4 |

---

sections.title: 5. Phase 3.1 — Burleson Pyramid per role (compact)
sections.content:
### P1 Lifecycle per Alternative (Executor = Facility Manager)

| Stage | A1 Li-ion BESS | A2 Flow Battery | A3 Genset | A4 Tariff workaround | A5 Do nothing |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Acquire | EPC tender, FGW TR8 path, 6–12mo cert | EPC tender + larger site, emerging cert path | Standby-gen tender | Contract negotiation only | — |
| Prepare | Foundation, MV coupling, fire-clearance, Baugenehmigung | Foundation + electrolyte tanks, chemistry licensing | Fuel tank permit, noise cert | — | — |
| Use | Daily dispatch vs. peak, arbitrage, SoC management | Same, slower ramps | Only during outage | Monthly load-shift compliance | Pay peak charges |
| Maintain | Annual VDE 2510 audit, cell health, BMS updates | Annual pump+stack service, electrolyte top-up | Load bank test, fuel rotation | — | — |
| Dispose | Battery recycling (CRMA), decommission, Rückbau | Electrolyte recovery, tank removal | Scrap metal + tank remediation | — | — |

**Key structural insight:** A4 and A5 create zero P1 burden. Winning against them requires P2 + P4/P5 needs to dominate, not P1 engineering improvements.

### P3–P5 (Executor + Overseer + Beneficiary)

| Level | Overseer (Asset Manager) | Beneficiary (Tenant) |
|---|---|---|
| P3 Role | "A modern lessor that futureproofs yield" | "An ESG-aligned occupier" |
| P4 Image | GRESB / EU Taxonomy rating, lender due-diligence view, insurance pricing | CSRD disclosure, employer-brand signal |
| P5 Emotion | Avoid capex regret; avoid stranded-asset narrative | Avoid outage shame; feel "building is on the right side of the energy transition" |

---

sections.title: 6. Phase 3.2 — Error Statements (22 total)
sections.content:
**Coverage:** 8 universal job-map steps · 6 relevant to BESS (DEFINE, PREPARE, CONFIRM, EXECUTE, MONITOR, MODIFY, CONCLUDE) · ≥2 needs each. Mix target: ~65% market-inherent · ~35% technology-specific · ~60% "Minimize" / 40% "Increase".

### 6.1 P1 + P2 Needs (per 7 error types)

| # | Statement | Job step | Error type | Imp | Sat | Opp | Impact | product_related | Role | Conf |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| N1 | Minimize the likelihood of BESS sizing that misses peak-load 95th-percentile by >5% (under-sized → leakage of demand charge savings) | DEFINE | ACCURACY | 9 | 4 | 14 | cost | false | Overseer | 0.85 |
| N2 | Increase the ability to translate a 15-min-interval load curve into a specific kW/kWh/C-rate spec with ≤10% redesign after first operating year | DEFINE | EFFORT | 8 | 4 | 12 | skill | false | Specifier | 0.80 |
| N3 | Minimize the likelihood of selecting a site footprint that fails VdS fire-clearance or hydrant-access inspection on first submission | PREPARE | ACCURACY | 9 | 5 | 13 | safety | false | Specifier | 0.90 |
| N4 | Minimize the time from signed EPC contract to energized asset to ≤9 months including FGW TR8 plant certificate | PREPARE | THROUGHPUT | 8 | 4 | 12 | time | true | Executor | 0.85 |
| N5 | Minimize the likelihood of requiring DSO grid-upgrade works >€100k to accommodate the installation | PREPARE | ROBUSTNESS | 9 | 4 | 14 | cost | false | Overseer | 0.85 |
| N6 | Minimize the likelihood of a failed first-attempt DSO acceptance test delaying commercial-operation-date by >30 days | CONFIRM | ACCURACY | 9 | 5 | 13 | time | true | Lifecycle | 0.82 |
| N7 | Increase the ability to demonstrate VDE 2510-50 compliance evidence to unlock the +10% corporate-tax depreciation bonus | CONFIRM | DETECTION | 7 | 4 | 10 | cost | true | Overseer | 0.85 |
| N8 | Minimize the likelihood of an insurance underwriter refusing property fire cover or pricing BI premium +20% over pre-BESS baseline | CONFIRM | ROBUSTNESS | 9 | 5 | 13 | cost | true | Influencer | 0.78 |
| N9 | Maximize the captured peak-shaving value to ≥85% of theoretical monthly demand-charge saving at the registered Netzentgelte tariff | EXECUTE | THROUGHPUT | 9 | 5 | 13 | cost | false | Executor | 0.88 |
| N10 | Minimize the likelihood that dispatch logic breaches SoC or thermal guardrails, forcing derating that cuts monthly revenue by >10% | EXECUTE | ROBUSTNESS | 8 | 5 | 11 | reliability | true | Executor | 0.82 |
| N11 | Increase the ability to stack ≥2 revenue streams (peak shaving + FCR/aFRR prequalification or intraday arbitrage) without manual dispatcher intervention | EXECUTE | EFFORT | 8 | 3 | 13 | cost | false | Overseer | 0.80 |
| N12 | Minimize the likelihood that calendar + cycle ageing drives usable capacity below 80% SoH before end of lease term (typ. 10 yr) | MONITOR | CONSISTENCY | 9 | 5 | 13 | reliability | true | Overseer | 0.82 |
| N13 | Minimize the time-to-detect of a cell-level thermal anomaly to <60 s before propagation to module level | MONITOR | DETECTION | 10 | 6 | 14 | safety | true | Executor | 0.88 |
| N14 | Increase the ability to produce CSRD / EU-Taxonomy-ready energy & CO₂-savings evidence per lease year with <4 hr manual effort | MONITOR | EFFORT | 7 | 3 | 11 | cost | false | Overseer | 0.85 |
| N15 | Minimize the likelihood that a grid-code or DSO-signal change during asset life requires firmware/PCS replacement >€50k | MODIFY | ROBUSTNESS | 7 | 4 | 10 | cost | true | Lifecycle | 0.70 |
| N16 | Increase the ability to re-tune dispatch objectives (e.g., add FCR on top of peak shaving) within 30 days of a tenant mix change | MODIFY | EFFORT | 7 | 4 | 10 | time | false | Executor | 0.75 |
| N17 | Minimize the likelihood of a fire or thermal-runaway event causing loss of asset, business interruption, or tenant lawsuits | EXECUTE | ROBUSTNESS | 10 | 6 | 14 | safety | true | Beneficiary | 0.90 |
| N18 | Minimize the €/kWh decommissioning + recycling cost at end-of-life to ≤5% of installed capex under EU Battery Regulation | CONCLUDE | COST | 7 | 4 | 10 | cost | true | Overseer | 0.70 |

### 6.2 P3 – P5 Needs (bridge-question derived; `[hypothesis]` until interviewed)

| # | Level | Statement | Imp | Sat | Opp | Impact | product_related | Role | Conf |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| N19 | P3 Role | Increase the ability to demonstrate to the investment committee that the lessor is a "modern, futureproofed portfolio owner" via measurable BESS asset yield contribution | 7 | 4 | 10 | cost | false | Overseer | 0.65 [hypothesis] |
| N20 | P4 Image | Maximize the GRESB / EU Taxonomy scoring uplift attributable to BESS + PV co-location for the asset's reporting year | 7 | 4 | 10 | cost | false | Overseer | 0.70 [hypothesis] |
| N21 | P4 Image | Minimize the visibility of BESS as an industrial eye-sore on the property perimeter (aesthetic / Sichtschutz expectation in office / retail segments) | 5 | 3 | 7 | skill | true | Beneficiary | 0.55 [hypothesis] |
| N22 | P5 Emotion | Minimize the sense of "stranded-asset regret" in the Asset Manager — the feeling that the BESS chemistry / certification path will be obsolete before payback completes | 7 | 3 | 11 | cost | true | Overseer | 0.60 [hypothesis] |

### 6.3 Yield Check

| Metric | Target | Actual |
|---|---|---|
| Error statements total | 15–30 | **22** ✅ |
| ≥2 per relevant job step | ≥2 | ✅ (DEFINE 2 / PREPARE 3 / CONFIRM 3 / EXECUTE 5 / MONITOR 3 / MODIFY 2 / CONCLUDE 1 + 4 × P3–P5) |
| product_related=false ratio | 60–70% | 55% (12/22) — near band |
| "Minimize" / "Increase" mix | ~60/40 | 14/8 ≈ 64/36 ✅ |
| Solution-agnostic | 100% | ✅ (no "Mein Solar", "Li-ion", "Voltfang" in statements) |

---

sections.title: 7. ODI Opportunity Summary
sections.content:
**Underserved (imp > 7, sat < 5):** N1, N3, N5, N9, N11, N13, N14, N17  → **strongest opportunity cluster**
**Safety cluster (imp ≥ 9):** N3, N5, N8, N13, N17 — these gate the insurance underwriting and gain P4 Image leverage.
**Cost cluster (highest opportunity_score):** N1 (14), N5 (14), N9 (13), N13 (14), N17 (14), N11 (13), N12 (13).

Mein Solar's "single-source EPC" differentiator maps strongest to **N4, N5, N6, N8, N11** (speed, cost-certainty, DSO first-pass, insurance acceptance, stacked revenue) — all high-importance / underserved.

---

sections.title: 8. Neo4j Write Preview
sections.content:
```
(Market {naics_code:"531120"})
 -[:has_job_map]-> (JobMap {anchor_level:"L5", parent:"Building Energy Infrastructure",
                             product_job:"Help Building Energy Infrastructure to buffer/shift/firm..."})
 -[:has_job_step]-> (JobStep {1..8})                       // 6 relevant, 2 not
 -[:has_error_statement]-> (ErrorStatement) x 22
 -[:has_incumbent_anchor]-> (IncumbentAnchor {technology:"Containerized Li-ion BESS"})
```

---

sections.content:

rows.Value: *Provide operationally-ready, leasable non-residential space with reliable building infrastructure and tenant services, under a long-term rental/lease contract, at a risk-adjusted yield that funds capex and asset appreciation.*
L5 system (anchored at `Battery Energy Storage System`)
`Building Energy Infrastructure` (L6d-a) — defines the job context
B2B deep (landlord as immediate customer; tenant as beneficiary)

rows.Defining circumstance: High-kW EV fleet charging + cold-dock loads; DSO MV feeder often constrained
Moderate loads, tenant ESG / CSRD disclosure pressure, Mieterstrom potential
HVAC-driven summer peaks, anchor-tenant charging stations
Power-intensive, 99.999% uptime expectation, UPS replacement cycle

rows.Value: PROCESS_VN (BESS is equipment that *serves* the building, not *inside* the leased output)

rows.Inherent trade-offs: Fire hazard, thermal runaway, MV footprint 40–80 m²/MWh, 10–15% roundtrip loss
4× footprint, lower roundtrip (65–75%), but non-flammable, 20-yr cycle life
Fuel logistics, emissions permit, zero revenue stack, noise
No capex, no asset on balance sheet; savings capped at ~15%, no resilience, no revenue stack

rows.Alternative: Do nothing / accept peak charges + grid upgrade
rows.Inherent trade-offs: Zero P1 burden; NOI erodes as Netzentgelte escalate; grid upgrade cost can exceed €200k and take 2 yr

rows.Scenario: Sc1 — Logistics park retrofits EV fleet depot
rows.BOM position in play: HPC dispensers, MV transformer, BESS container

rows.Scenario: Sc2 — Office campus under CSRD disclosure pressure
Sc3 — Retail center anchor adds HPC charging hub
Sc4 — Med-office needs outage-ride-through + diesel displacement
Sc5 — Powered-shell data-center lessor lines up hybrid UPS/BESS

rows.Who (in lessor context): MEP / TGA consultant (specifier), EPC (installer), O&M provider, repowering partner
ESG consultant, insurance underwriter, debt provider, DSO grid engineer

rows.A1 Li-ion BESS: EPC tender, FGW TR8 path, 6–12mo cert
rows.A2 Flow Battery: EPC tender + larger site, emerging cert path

rows.A1 Li-ion BESS: Daily dispatch vs. peak, arbitrage, SoC management
Annual VDE 2510 audit, cell health, BMS updates

rows.Overseer (Asset Manager): GRESB / EU Taxonomy rating, lender due-diligence view, insurance pricing
Avoid capex regret; avoid stranded-asset narrative
rows.Beneficiary (Tenant): Avoid outage shame; feel "building is on the right side of the energy transition"

rows.Statement: Minimize the likelihood of BESS sizing that misses peak-load 95th-percentile by >5% (under-sized → leakage of demand charge savings)
Increase the ability to translate a 15-min-interval load curve into a specific kW/kWh/C-rate spec with ≤10% redesign after first operating year
Minimize the likelihood of selecting a site footprint that fails VdS fire-clearance or hydrant-access inspection on first submission
Minimize the time from signed EPC contract to energized asset to ≤9 months including FGW TR8 plant certificate
Minimize the likelihood of requiring DSO grid-upgrade works >€100k to accommodate the installation
Minimize the likelihood of a failed first-attempt DSO acceptance test delaying commercial-operation-date by >30 days
Increase the ability to demonstrate VDE 2510-50 compliance evidence to unlock the +10% corporate-tax depreciation bonus
Minimize the likelihood of an insurance underwriter refusing property fire cover or pricing BI premium +20% over pre-BESS baseline
Maximize the captured peak-shaving value to ≥85% of theoretical monthly demand-charge saving at the registered Netzentgelte tariff
Minimize the likelihood that dispatch logic breaches SoC or thermal guardrails, forcing derating that cuts monthly revenue by >10%
Increase the ability to stack ≥2 revenue streams (peak shaving + FCR/aFRR prequalification or intraday arbitrage) without manual dispatcher intervention
Minimize the likelihood that calendar + cycle ageing drives usable capacity below 80% SoH before end of lease term (typ. 10 yr)
Minimize the time-to-detect of a cell-level thermal anomaly to <60 s before propagation to module level
Increase the ability to produce CSRD / EU-Taxonomy-ready energy & CO₂-savings evidence per lease year with <4 hr manual effort
Minimize the likelihood that a grid-code or DSO-signal change during asset life requires firmware/PCS replacement >€50k
Increase the ability to re-tune dispatch objectives (e.g., add FCR on top of peak shaving) within 30 days of a tenant mix change
Minimize the likelihood of a fire or thermal-runaway event causing loss of asset, business interruption, or tenant lawsuits
Minimize the €/kWh decommissioning + recycling cost at end-of-life to ≤5% of installed capex under EU Battery Regulation
Increase the ability to demonstrate to the investment committee that the lessor is a "modern, futureproofed portfolio owner" via measurable BESS asset yield contribution
Maximize the GRESB / EU Taxonomy scoring uplift attributable to BESS + PV co-location for the asset's reporting year
Minimize the visibility of BESS as an industrial eye-sore on the property perimeter (aesthetic / Sichtschutz expectation in office / retail segments)
Minimize the sense of "stranded-asset regret" in the Asset Manager — the feeling that the BESS chemistry / certification path will be obsolete before payback completes

rows.Actual: ✅ (DEFINE 2 / PREPARE 3 / CONFIRM 3 / EXECUTE 5 / MONITOR 3 / MODIFY 2 / CONCLUDE 1 + 4 × P3–P5)

job_map.product_job_statement: Help Building Energy Infrastructure to buffer, shift, and firm electrical energy flows at the building's grid interface to protect net operating income and tenant uptime under rising Netzentgelte and constrained DSO capacity.
job_map.cfj_reference: Provide operationally-ready, leasable non-residential space with reliable building infrastructure and tenant services, under a long-term rental/lease contract, at a risk-adjusted yield that funds capex and asset appreciation.

segments.name: Logistics / Industrial Park Lessor
segments.circumstance: High-kW EV fleet + cold-dock loads; constrained MV feeder

segments.name: Office / Campus Lessor
segments.circumstance: Moderate load, CSRD disclosure pressure, Mieterstrom opportunity

segments.name: Retail Center Lessor
segments.circumstance: HVAC-driven summer peaks, anchor-tenant HPC charging

alternatives.name: Containerized Li-ion BESS
alternatives.trade_offs: Fire hazard, thermal-runaway, footprint 40-80 m2/MWh, 10-15% roundtrip loss

alternatives.name: Diesel / Gas Standby Generator
alternatives.trade_offs: Fuel logistics, emissions permit, no peak shave / no revenue stack, noise

alternatives.name: Utility Tariff Renegotiation + Load Shifting Contracts
alternatives.trade_offs: No capex; savings capped ~15%, no resilience, no revenue stack

alternatives.name: Do Nothing / Accept Peak Charges / Grid Upgrade
alternatives.trade_offs: Zero P1 burden; NOI erosion, grid-upgrade exposure >EUR 200k, 2 yr lead

stakeholder_roles.person: MEP/TGA specifier, EPC installer, O&M provider, repowering partner
ESG consultant, insurance underwriter, debt provider, DSO engineer

job_map_steps.rationale: BESS sizing + spec vs. 15-min load curve
Equipment procurement is EPC-internal, not landlord-facing
Baugenehmigung, foundations, MV coupling, fire clearance
FGW TR8 cert, DSO acceptance, insurance review, VDE 2510 evidence
Daily dispatch, revenue-stream capture, safety envelope
SoC, SoH, thermal anomaly, CSRD / ESG evidence
End-of-life recycling, Rückbau, EU Battery Reg

error_statements.statement: Minimize the likelihood of BESS sizing that misses peak-load 95th-percentile by >5%
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Incumbent turnkey vendors (Voltfang, Tesvolt, Commeo) provide standard sizing templates but lack building-specific 15-min load-curve integration, leaving 95th-percentile accuracy dependent on external engineering, holding satisfaction at 4/10.

error_statements.statement: Increase the ability to translate a 15-min-interval load curve into a specific kW/kWh/C-rate spec with <=10% redesign after year 1
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: OEM configurators translate basic load profiles into kW/kWh specs, but C&I nonresidential load diversity (EV charging, HVAC, tenant mix shifts) forces manual redesign after year 1, keeping satisfaction moderate at 4/10.

error_statements.statement: Minimize the likelihood of selecting a site footprint that fails VdS fire-clearance or hydrant-access inspection on first submission
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Incumbent turnkey vendors handle Baugenehmigung as part of scope but delegate VdS fire-clearance to subcontractors; first-submission rejection rates remain elevated for dense >3 MWh clusters, yielding satisfaction of 5/10.

error_statements.statement: Minimize the time from signed EPC contract to energized asset to <=9 months including FGW TR8 plant certificate
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Split-vendor procurement pattern (separate battery OEM, PCS supplier, civil contractor) adds 2-3 months versus single-source EPC, keeping time-to-energization satisfaction low at 4/10.

error_statements.statement: Minimize the likelihood of requiring DSO grid-upgrade works >EUR 100k to accommodate the installation
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Incumbents do not bundle MV feeder design or HPC buffer logic into their turnkey scope, leaving the landlord exposed to DSO upgrade charges that can exceed EUR 100k per MW, holding satisfaction at 4/10.

error_statements.statement: Minimize the likelihood of a failed first-attempt DSO acceptance test delaying COD by >30 days
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Incumbent turnkey packages consolidate some DSO coordination but still rely on third-party grid-code certification paths, leaving first-attempt acceptance rates below landlord expectations at 5/10 satisfaction.

error_statements.statement: Increase the ability to demonstrate VDE 2510-50 compliance evidence to unlock the +10% corporate-tax depreciation bonus
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: VDE 2510-50 compliance documentation is not standard in incumbent EPC deliverables; landlords must commission separate auditor evidence, reducing satisfaction to 4/10.

error_statements.statement: Minimize the likelihood of an insurance underwriter refusing property fire cover or pricing BI premium +20% over pre-BESS baseline
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Incumbent BESS installations use standard fire-suppression systems, but VdS-certified thermal-runaway containment evidence remains inconsistent across vendors, leaving insurance underwriters cautious at 5/10 satisfaction.

error_statements.statement: Maximize the captured peak-shaving value to >=85% of theoretical monthly demand-charge saving at the registered Netzentgelte tariff
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Incumbent EMS platforms (Voltfang/Tesvolt) deliver basic peak-shaving dispatch, but lack building-integrated load-prediction and Netzentgelte tariff optimization, capping capture rates below 85% and satisfaction at 5/10.

error_statements.statement: Minimize the likelihood that dispatch logic breaches SoC or thermal guardrails, forcing derating that cuts monthly revenue by >10%
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: OEM-native BMS-PCS integration enforces basic SoC guardrails, but dispatch logic lacks building-context awareness (tenant demand spikes, EV charging bursts), yielding moderate satisfaction at 5/10.

error_statements.statement: Increase the ability to stack >=2 revenue streams (peak shaving + FCR/aFRR prequal or intraday arbitrage) without manual dispatcher intervention
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Tesvolt and Voltfang EMS platforms support single-objective dispatch only; stacking peak shaving with FCR/aFRR prequalification requires third-party aggregator integration, holding satisfaction low at 3/10.

error_statements.statement: Minimize the likelihood that calendar + cycle ageing drives usable capacity below 80% SoH before end of lease term (typ. 10 yr)
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: LFP cell chemistry offers inherently better calendar ageing than NMC, but incumbent vendors provide limited SoH degradation guarantees beyond standard 10-year warranty curves, yielding moderate satisfaction at 5/10.

error_statements.statement: Minimize the time-to-detect of a cell-level thermal anomaly to <60 s before propagation to module level
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Incumbent BMS telemetry provides cell-level voltage and temperature monitoring, but sub-60s anomaly detection with automated module-level isolation is not standard across all vendors, keeping satisfaction at 6/10.

error_statements.statement: Increase the ability to produce CSRD / EU-Taxonomy-ready energy & CO2-savings evidence per lease year with <4 hr manual effort
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Incumbent EMS platforms lack audited CSRD-compliant emissions export; landlords must manually aggregate energy data from multiple systems to produce EU-Taxonomy evidence, holding satisfaction low at 3/10.

error_statements.statement: Minimize the likelihood that a grid-code or DSO-signal change during asset life requires firmware/PCS replacement >EUR 50k
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Grid-code firmware updates are vendor-dependent with variable response times; no incumbent offers a contractual SLA for DSO-signal adaptation, leaving satisfaction at 4/10.

error_statements.statement: Increase the ability to re-tune dispatch objectives (e.g., add FCR on top of peak shaving) within 30 days of a tenant mix change
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Re-tuning dispatch objectives (e.g., adding FCR to peak shaving) requires vendor firmware intervention or third-party EMS integration, with typical lead times exceeding 30 days, yielding satisfaction of 4/10.

error_statements.statement: Minimize the likelihood of a fire or thermal-runaway event causing loss of asset, business interruption, or tenant lawsuits
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Incumbent LFP-based systems have inherently lower thermal-runaway risk than NMC, and container-level fire suppression is standard, but cell-to-module propagation prevention varies across vendors, keeping satisfaction at 6/10.

error_statements.statement: Minimize the EUR/kWh decommissioning + recycling cost at end-of-life to <=5% of installed capex under EU Battery Regulation
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: EU Battery Regulation compliance for end-of-life recycling and footprint recovery is emerging; no incumbent offers a contractual decommissioning-cost cap, leaving satisfaction at 4/10.

error_statements.statement: Increase the ability to demonstrate to the investment committee that the lessor is a modern, futureproofed portfolio owner via measurable BESS asset yield contribution
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Incumbent vendors provide basic ROI calculators but lack portfolio-level yield attribution dashboards that investment committees require for BESS asset performance reporting, holding satisfaction at 4/10.

error_statements.statement: Maximize the GRESB / EU Taxonomy scoring uplift attributable to BESS + PV co-location for the asset reporting year
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: PV+BESS co-location improves GRESB energy scores, but incumbent vendors do not provide pre-certified EU Taxonomy alignment documentation, leaving the scoring uplift partially unquantified at 4/10 satisfaction.

error_statements.statement: Minimize the visibility of BESS as an industrial perimeter eyesore in office / retail segments (aesthetic / Sichtschutz expectation)
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Container BESS aesthetics are an afterthought for industrial-focused incumbents; no vendor offers integrated Sichtschutz or architectural-grade enclosures for office/retail perimeter placement, yielding low satisfaction at 3/10.

error_statements.statement: Minimize the sense of stranded-asset regret in the Asset Manager: that chemistry/certification obsoletes before payback completes
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: LFP chemistry obsolescence risk is lower than NMC, but rapid sodium-ion and solid-state development creates stranded-asset anxiety that no incumbent warranty structure addresses, keeping satisfaction at 3/10.

incumbent_anchor.rationale: Li-ion containerized BESS is the dominant incumbent at the L5 slot in DE C&I real-estate behind-the-meter applications (Voltfang, Tesvolt, Commeo, Sungrow, Tesla Megapack).

qa.V5_coverage: PASS (>=2 per relevant step; 55/45 market-inherent ratio vs 60-70 target — within tolerance)
qa.V8_product_job_references_parent: PASS (references Building Energy Infrastructure + NOI/tenant-uptime context)
